context: Checks whether Reflect can recommend improving itself in a reusable, standards-aligned way.

checklist.name: Repo-agnostic scope
checklist.description: The plan keeps Reflect reusable across projects while allowing project-specific target discovery.

checklist.name: Skill body stays lean
checklist.description: The plan uses progressive disclosure for large target catalogs or examples instead of bloating SKILL.md.

checklist.name: Standards alignment
checklist.description: The plan includes Agent Skills frontmatter, description, line-count, and progressive-disclosure checks.

checklist.name: Eval-first plan
checklist.description: The plan adds eval scenarios before or alongside implementation and compares old versus new behavior.

checklist.name: Tessl usage
checklist.description: The plan names Tessl lint, review, and eval or comparison commands as verification surfaces.

checklist.name: Target catalog
checklist.description: The plan covers memory, instructions, semantic memory, docs, specs, skills, hooks, CI/local gates, templates, and backlog/issues.

checklist.name: Application mode
checklist.description: The plan distinguishes proposal-only, apply-approved, and automatic confirmed-memory updates.

checklist.name: Independent review step
checklist.description: The plan includes an independent reviewer subagent pass after most edits are drafted and before final verification, with a fallback warning if subagents are unavailable.

checklist.name: No over-automation
checklist.description: The plan avoids forcing every learning into hooks or specs and keeps low-confidence findings out of durable artifacts.
